item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents Wil Blades, Herlin Riley, Will Bernard and Donald Harrison Play the Music of Dr. Lonnie Smith
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-04-28T23:00:00.000-04:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.showpass.com/wil-blades-music-of-dr-lonnie-smith-bbjfs/ Tickets: https://www.showpass.com/wil-blades-music-of-dr-lonnie-smith-bbjfs/
Tuesday April 28
Doors 9:00PM / Show 10:00PM
Advance Tickets $25 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Jazz organ authority, the legendary Dr. Lonnie Smith has called Wil Blades “the future,” anointing him the heir apparent to “carry on the legend [and] the legacy of the B-3 organ.”
All sales are final and refunds are only allowed in limited circumstances such as canceled events. If your event is canceled, postponed, rescheduled, or moved, we will attempt to contact you to update you on the status of the event, and inform you of any refund, credit, or exchange procedures.
All shows are for ages 21 and up. Valid ID required upon entry.

This ain’t your Grandma’s Bingo —

2026 GRAMMY NOMINEES! New Breed Brass Band is the new sound of New Orleans, and its members have learned from the city's best including Troy “Trombone Shorty” Andrews and James “12” Andrews. New Breed takes the NOLA second-line brass band tradition to all-new places.

Comedy Gumbeaux is a New Orleans Comedy institution. Local, national, and even international comedians have graced our stage, and yet we have space in our “Rising Stars” section for anyone who is brave enough to take the mic. item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents Flow Tribe
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-04-30T22:30:00.000-05:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.showpass.com/flow-tribe-blue-nile-april-30-bbjfs/ Tickets: https://www.showpass.com/flow-tribe-blue-nile-april-30-bbjfs/
Thursday April 30
Doors 9:30PM / Show 10:30PM
Advance Tickets $20 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Founded in 2004, the band solidified in the wake of Hurricane Katrina's destruction with the mission to bring the next generation of the New Orleans' sound of unstoppable rhythm and passion to the world. The next 15 years would find Flow Tribe performing over 120 shows a year crossing the country multiple times from the biggest festival stages to freak shows. These are six seasoned blue collar musicians who grind out the good times. They carry on the road dog tradition of following the highway to wherever the next crowd congregates.

All shows are for ages 21 and up. Valid ID required upon entry.

item.title: Mitch Woods Club 88 New Orleans Jazzfest residency at Creole Gardens Roadhouse. I’m inviting all my musician friends to sit in and the public to make this a Jazzfest to remember!
item.description:
Venue: Creole Gardens Guest House Starts: 2026-05-01T19:00:00.000-06:00 Categories: Music - Classical, Music - Other, Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Jazz & Blues More info: http://www.mitchwoods.com
Mitch Woods invites all his friends, musical and otherwise to join him in his yearly Jazzfest Residency at the classic New Orleans Roadhouse @ Creole Gardens Guest House and Inn.
I’m inviting all my musician friends to sit in and the public to make this a Jazzfest to remember.
There will be bar and food available. Backline too.
C’mon down each night 8-10 pm after the fest and before the rest!

item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents Kermit Ruffins & the BBQ Swingers
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-01T22:30:00.000-05:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.bluenilelive.com/backbeat-jazz-fest-series-2026 Tickets: https://www.bluenilelive.com/backbeat-jazz-fest-series-2026
Friday April 24
Doors 9:30PM / Show 10:30PM
Advance Tickets $30 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Day of show $35 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
“We Partyin’!”
Kermit Ruffins needs only two words to hype a New Orleans barroom for one of his weekly shows, and this, his personal slogan, sums up what this Big Easy ambassador is all about. From playing himself in the HBO Series Treme, to barbecuing outside his bar, Kermit’s Treme Mother-inLaw Lounge, to sitting in with Jon Batiste and Stay Human on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, the trumpeter/vocalist keeps himself busy when he isn’t on stage doing what he does best—entertaining and sharing his love of life with the world. He personifies the laid-back vibe of New Orleans.
All sales are final and refunds are only allowed in limited circumstances such as canceled events. If your event is canceled, postponed, rescheduled, or moved, we will attempt to contact you to update you on the status of the event, and inform you of any refund, credit, or exchange procedures.

item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents lespecial: Tentacle Difficulties Tour with special guest Mike Dillon
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-01T23:45:00.000-05:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.showpass.com/lespecial-blue-nile-april-2026-bbjfs/
LATE SHOW Friday May 1
Doors 12:30AM / Show 1:00AM
(This is a LATE SHOW Friday May 1. Technically this show starts early morning May 2.)
Advance Tickets $25 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Day of show $30 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
lespecial is redefining the term “power trio”. The multi-instrumentalists from Connecticut continue to push the boundaries of what a three-piece band is capable of both live and in the studio ahead of their latest album, “Odd Times”. The band’s signature blend of “heavy future groove” combines headbanging metal riffage and surgical rhythmic precision with bone shaking 808s, sub synths and ethereal vocal stylings for a dance floor that welcomes moshing, dancing and hip swaying alike. Listeners are taken on a journey to the musical netherworld through esoteric soundscapes punctuated by raw, primal power.
Odd Times, lespecial’s darkest and heaviest record to date, is an enigmatic and compelling musical project that explores the ever-changing nature of time amidst isolation. With heavy riffs, tribal drumming, and hints of levity, it's a captivating journey crafted in collaboration with Havok's David Sanchez. An intriguing blend of old-school metal and modern djent-inspired riffage awaits in this haunting sonic experience.
